patch.value: Declare trusted=true, sandbox_profile, and permissions on all 9 first-party package manifests

All 9 first-party package manifests (pm-beads, pm-calendar, pm-governance-audit, pm-guide-shell, pm-lifecycle-hooks, pm-linked-test-adapters, pm-search-advanced, pm-templates, pm-todos) are missing `trusted`, `sandbox_profile`, and `permissions` fields. These are the very features that extension governance policy evaluates. First-party packages serve as exemplars for third-party authors — if they don't declare these fields, authors copy the pattern and produce unsigned, unprofileable packages. The governance-audit manifest in particular should set sandbox_profile since it intentionally hooks onRead/onWrite. Evidence: all 9 manifests confirmed via audit 2026-06-07.

- All 9 first-party manifests declare `trusted`, `sandbox_profile`, and `permissions`
- governance-audit manifest declares a sandbox_profile consistent with its onRead/onWrite hooks
- Governance test enforces presence of these fields on first-party manifests
